data.requirement: Extract and aggregate data based on customer_status categories (Stayed, Churned, Joined) for key metrics (satisfaction_score, churn_score, CLTV).
data.reason: Customer status is the primary analysis entity, and comparisons across key metrics are necessary to analyze behavior patterns and differences in these groups.

data.requirement: Include demographic data (age, gender, senior_citizen, dependents, married) in the analysis, segmented by customer_status.
data.reason: Demographic characteristics provide contextual information to identify trends and potential explanations for churn or retention behavior for different customer groups.

data.requirement: Provide an overview of the distribution of numerical metrics (satisfaction_score, churn_score, CLTV) by customer_status, including statistics such as mean, median, and variance.
data.reason: This ensures that macro-level trends and differences can be observed and contrasted across groups.

data.requirement: Enable the identification of extreme values (high or low churn_scores, satisfaction_scores, CLTV) within each customer_status group.
data.reason: Analyzing outliers can provide deeper insights into factors affecting unusually high/low performances, churn, or retention.

data.requirement: Incorporate and join data from the Service_Options and Online_Services tables based on customer_id to examine how service usage (internet_service, phone_service, streaming services, etc.) clusters with customer_status.
data.reason: Service usage patterns may highlight specific behaviors or products influencing churn or retention.

data.requirement: Guide users from an aggregated view of all customer groups to a detailed inspection of individual customer profiles and their service/demographic metrics.
data.reason: This drill-down capability enables deeper examination of individual cases and ensures logical flow from general to specific insights.

visualization.requirement: Utilize bar charts to compare the mean satisfaction_score, churn_score, and CLTV for each customer_status group.
visualization.reason: Bar charts are effective for group-level comparisons and showing differences in these metrics across customer statuses.

visualization.requirement: Create box plots for satisfaction_score, churn_score, and CLTV segmented by customer_status.
visualization.reason: Box plots allow visualization of the spread, medians, and outliers within each group for these key metrics.

visualization.requirement: Use a heat map to depict correlations between service usage variables (e.g., online_security, premium_tech_support) and customer_status.
visualization.reason: Heat maps effectively reveal patterns and potential factors driving differences in customer behavior.

visualization.requirement: Deploy a stacked bar chart to show the proportions of demographic characteristics (e.g., gender, senior_citizen) within each status group.
visualization.reason: Stacked bar charts are suited for showing proportions and categorical breakdowns within groups for demographic comparisons.

visualization.requirement: Create a scatter plot for churn_score vs. CLTV, color-coded by customer_status, with optional point size based on satisfaction_score.
visualization.reason: Scatter plots support correlation and trend analysis to investigate interactions between churn risk, customer value, and customer satisfaction.

visualization.requirement: Implement a line chart or area chart to trend satisfaction_score or churn_score over tenure for different customer_status groups.
visualization.reason: These visualizations are useful for understanding temporal patterns and changes in satisfaction or churn likelihood over time.

interaction.requirement: Allow filtering of data by customer_status (Stayed, Churned, Joined) using dropdown menus or buttons.
interaction.reason: Users need the ability to isolate and focus on specific customer groups to perform comparative analyses or drill-downs.

interaction.requirement: Enable selection or brushing on visualizations (e.g., box plots or scatter plots) to highlight specific ranges or points, dynamically updating related views.
interaction.reason: Helps users understand relationships between selected data points and the broader dataset.

interaction.requirement: Integrate tooltips to display customer-level details (e.g., satisfaction_score, churn_score, CLTV, demographics) when hovering over visualization points (e.g., scatter plot points or heat map cells).
interaction.reason: Allows the user to access detailed information when needed, without overwhelming the interface with data at all times.

interaction.requirement: Provide drill-down capabilities from macro-level group summaries (e.g., heat map or bar chart) to individual customer records via clicking.
interaction.reason: Supports logical navigation from general insights to specific, actionable cases for further analysis.

interaction.requirement: Allow selection of specific demographic features (e.g., gender, senior_citizen) to highlight or compare distributions within the visualizations.
interaction.reason: This interaction enables targeted subgroup analyses based on demographic characteristics.

interaction.requirement: Allow panning or zooming on scatter plots and line charts to analyze specific areas of interest (e.g., extreme CLTV values or churn_score trends).
interaction.reason: Panning and zooming assist in detailed examination of data regions otherwise difficult to observe in crowded plots or with wide data ranges.
